solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int tribonacci(int n) {
        if (n == 0) return 0;
        if (n == 1 || n == 2) return 1;

        int a = 0, b = 1, c = 1;
        for (int i = 3; i <= n; i++) {
            int next = a + b + c;
            a = b;
            b = c;
            c = next;
        }
        return c;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses an iterative approach with constant space to compute the Tribonacci sequence. It avoids recursion and memoization, making it efficient for the given constraint (n ≤ 37). The main strength is its linear time and constant space usage, while the weakness is that it recomputes values for repeated calls with the same input.
